name: Egg, whole, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Egg, whole, cooked); FODMAP: eggs contain no fructose/fructans (Cleveland Clinic low-FODMAP list)

name: Egg white, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Egg white, cooked); FODMAP: no fructose/fructans

name: Chicken breast, cooked, skinless
source: USDA FDC (Chicken, breast, cooked, roasted); FODMAP: plain meats are free of fructose/fructans (Monash/dietitian low-FODMAP guidance)

name: Rotisserie chicken, meat only
source: USDA FDC (rotisserie chicken, approximate); FODMAP unknown: seasoning/brine commonly contains onion/garlic powder (high fructans) so not verified safe

name: Turkey breast, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Turkey breast, roasted); FODMAP: plain poultry free of fructose/fructans

name: Ground turkey, cooked (turkey burger)
source: USDA FDC (Turkey, ground, cooked); FODMAP: plain ground turkey free of fructose/fructans

name: Turkey bacon, cooked
source: USDA FDC (turkey bacon, approximate); FODMAP unknown: cured products often contain garlic/onion powder and sweeteners; not verified safe

name: Low-sodium deli turkey
source: USDA FDC (deli turkey, approximate); FODMAP unknown: deli meats frequently include garlic/onion flavorings; not verified safe

name: Lean ground beef, cooked (90/10)
source: USDA FDC (Beef, ground, 90% lean, cooked); FODMAP: plain beef free of fructose/fructans

name: Lean ground pork, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Pork, ground, cooked); FODMAP: plain pork free of fructose/fructans

name: Salmon, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Salmon, Atlantic, cooked); FODMAP: plain fish free of fructose/fructans

name: Smoked salmon
source: USDA FDC (Fish, salmon, smoked); FODMAP: plain cured salmon free of fructose/fructans (high sodium flag for DASH)

name: Cod, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Fish, cod, cooked); FODMAP: plain fish free of fructose/fructans

name: Tilapia, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Fish, tilapia, cooked); FODMAP: plain fish free of fructose/fructans

name: Tuna, canned in water, drained
source: USDA FDC (Fish, tuna, light, canned in water); FODMAP: plain tuna free of fructose/fructans

name: Shrimp, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Crustaceans, shrimp, cooked); FODMAP: plain shellfish free of fructose/fructans

name: Firm tofu, drained
source: USDA FDC (Tofu, firm); FODMAP: firm/drained tofu is low FODMAP (Cleveland Clinic; Healthline FODMAP 101). Silken tofu is high - this row is FIRM only

name: Plain low-fat Greek yogurt
source: USDA FDC (Yogurt, Greek, plain, lowfat); FODMAP: Greek yogurt is low in fructose/fructans (lactose is out of scope for this user)

name: Feta cheese
source: USDA FDC (Cheese, feta); FODMAP: hard/aged cheeses are low fructose/fructans (Monash/dietitian lists)

name: Cheddar cheese, reduced-fat
source: USDA FDC (Cheese, cheddar, reduced fat); FODMAP: aged cheese low fructose/fructans

name: Mozzarella cheese, part-skim
source: USDA FDC (Cheese, mozzarella, part skim); FODMAP: mozzarella low fructose/fructans

name: Parmesan cheese, grated
source: USDA FDC (Cheese, parmesan, grated); FODMAP: aged cheese low fructose/fructans

name: Chickpeas, canned, drained
source: USDA FDC (Chickpeas, canned, drained); FODMAP: canned chickpeas low only at ~1/4 cup; fructans rise quickly with portion (dietitian low-FODMAP guidance). GOS is the main trigger but out of scope; marked moderate conservatively

name: Black beans, canned, drained
source: USDA FDC (Beans, black, canned); FODMAP: low only at small (~1/4 cup) portions; fructans/GOS rise with serving. Marked moderate conservatively (GOS out of scope)

name: Lentils, canned, drained
source: USDA FDC (Lentils, canned); FODMAP: canned lentils low only at ~1/4 cup; fructans/GOS rise with portion. Marked moderate conservatively (GOS out of scope)

name: Edamame, shelled, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Edamame, cooked); FODMAP: edamame low FODMAP at 1/2 cup (dietitian low-FODMAP lists). Low-FODMAP high-fiber option

name: Hummus
source: USDA FDC (Hummus, commercial); FODMAP: chickpea-based and frequently contains garlic (high fructans); marked moderate conservatively

name: Peanut butter
source: USDA FDC (Peanut butter, smooth); FODMAP: peanuts/peanut butter low FODMAP (Healthline FODMAP 101; dietitian lists)

name: Peanuts, dry roasted
source: USDA FDC (Peanuts, dry roasted); FODMAP: peanuts low FODMAP at 32 nut serving (dietitian low-FODMAP lists)

name: Almond butter
source: USDA FDC (Almond butter); FODMAP: 1 tbsp almond butter low FODMAP; larger (>~2 tbsp) becomes high (dietitian lists)

name: Almonds
source: USDA FDC (Nuts, almonds); FODMAP: 10 almonds (~12 g) low FODMAP; 20+ becomes high in fructans/GOS (dietitian low-FODMAP lists)

name: Walnuts
source: USDA FDC (Nuts, walnuts, English); FODMAP: walnuts low FODMAP (Healthline FODMAP 101; dietitian lists)

name: Pumpkin seeds (pepitas)
source: USDA FDC (Seeds, pumpkin/pepitas); FODMAP: pumpkin seeds low FODMAP at 2 tbsp (dietitian low-FODMAP lists)

name: Slivered almonds
source: USDA FDC (Nuts, almonds); FODMAP: small almond serving low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Chia seeds
source: USDA FDC (Seeds, chia); FODMAP: chia seeds low FODMAP at 2 tbsp (dietitian low-FODMAP lists). Flagship low-FODMAP high-fiber food

name: Flax seeds, ground
source: USDA FDC (Seeds, flaxseed); FODMAP: flax low FODMAP at 1 tbsp (dietitian low-FODMAP lists)

name: Hemp hearts
source: USDA FDC (Seeds, hemp); FODMAP: hemp hearts/seeds low FODMAP (dietitian low-FODMAP lists)

name: Tahini
source: USDA FDC (Seeds, sesame butter/tahini); FODMAP: tahini low FODMAP at 1 tbsp (dietitian low-FODMAP lists)

name: Cashews
source: FODMAP: cashews are high in fructans/GOS even at small servings (dietitian low-FODMAP lists; Healthline). Listed so the app flags them

name: Pistachios
source: FODMAP: pistachios high in fructans/GOS (dietitian low-FODMAP lists; Healthline). Listed so the app flags them

name: Olive oil, extra-virgin
source: USDA FDC (Oil, olive); FODMAP: pure oils contain no fructose/fructans (Monash/dietitian guidance)

name: Avocado oil
source: USDA FDC (Oil, avocado); FODMAP: pure oil has no fructose/fructans

name: Coconut oil
source: USDA FDC (Oil, coconut); FODMAP: pure oil has no fructose/fructans (high saturated fat flag for DASH)

name: Avocado
source: USDA FDC (Avocado, raw); FODMAP: avocado low FODMAP at 1/8 (~30 g); larger servings high in sorbitol (polyol, out of scope) but fructose/fructans stay low at this portion (dietitian lists)

name: Guacamole
source: USDA FDC (avocado base); FODMAP: guacamole commonly contains onion/garlic (high fructans); marked moderate conservatively

name: Black olives
source: USDA FDC (Olives, ripe, canned); FODMAP: olives low FODMAP (dietitian low-FODMAP lists)

name: Mayonnaise
source: USDA FDC (Mayonnaise); FODMAP: plain mayo (egg/oil/vinegar) low FODMAP (dietitian lists). Garlic-flavored versions would differ

name: Pesto
source: FODMAP: traditional pesto contains garlic (high fructans); marked high. Listed so the app flags meal-plan pesto dishes

name: Balsamic vinegar
source: USDA FDC (Vinegar, balsamic); FODMAP: balsamic vinegar low FODMAP at 1 tbsp; 2+ tbsp becomes high in fructose (dietitian lists)

name: Balsamic vinaigrette dressing
source: USDA FDC (balsamic vinaigrette, approximate); FODMAP unknown: commercial dressings vary and often contain garlic/onion/HFCS; not verified safe

name: White wine vinegar
source: USDA FDC (Vinegar, distilled/white wine); FODMAP: plain vinegar low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Lemon juice
source: USDA FDC (Lemon juice, raw); FODMAP: lemon juice low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Lime juice
source: USDA FDC (Lime juice, raw); FODMAP: lime juice low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Soy sauce
source: USDA FDC (Soy sauce); FODMAP: soy sauce low FODMAP at 2 tbsp (wheat-based but fructans below threshold) per dietitian lists. High sodium flag for DASH

name: Dijon mustard
source: USDA FDC (Mustard, prepared); FODMAP: mustard low FODMAP at 1 tbsp (dietitian lists)

name: Salsa
source: FODMAP: most commercial salsa contains onion and garlic (high fructans) and sometimes added sugar; marked high conservatively

name: Marinara/tomato sauce
source: FODMAP: jarred marinara usually contains onion/garlic (high fructans) and added sugars; marked high conservatively

name: Curry powder
source: USDA FDC (Curry powder); FODMAP: pure curry powder low FODMAP at 1 tsp (dietitian lists); blends with onion/garlic powder would differ

name: Smoked paprika
source: USDA FDC (Paprika); FODMAP: paprika low FODMAP at 1 tsp (dietitian lists)

name: Cinnamon, ground
source: USDA FDC (Spices, cinnamon, ground); FODMAP: cinnamon low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Garlic, raw
source: FODMAP: garlic is one of the highest-fructan foods, high even at one clove (Monash/Cleveland Clinic/Healthline high-FODMAP lists). Primary offender to flag

name: Garlic powder
source: FODMAP: garlic powder is concentrated fructans, high even in small amounts (dietitian high-FODMAP lists). Primary offender to flag

name: Onion, raw
source: FODMAP: onion is a top fructan source, high even in small amounts (Monash/Cleveland Clinic/Healthline). Primary offender to flag

name: Scallion/green onion, green tops only
source: FODMAP: GREEN tops of scallions are low FODMAP; the white bulb is high in fructans (dietitian low-FODMAP lists). This row is green tops only

name: Scallion/green onion, white part
source: FODMAP: the white bulb of scallions is high in fructans like onion (dietitian lists). Flagged so recipes calling for whole scallions are caught

name: Spinach, raw
source: USDA FDC (Spinach, raw); FODMAP: baby/raw spinach low FODMAP (dietitian low-FODMAP lists)

name: Spinach, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Spinach, boiled, drained); FODMAP: spinach low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Kale, raw
source: USDA FDC (Kale, raw); FODMAP: kale low FODMAP at ~1 cup (dietitian low-FODMAP lists)

name: Romaine lettuce
source: USDA FDC (Lettuce, romaine); FODMAP: lettuce low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Butter/Boston bibb lettuce
source: USDA FDC (Lettuce, butterhead); FODMAP: lettuce low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Mixed salad greens
source: USDA FDC (mixed greens, approximate); FODMAP: common salad greens (lettuce, spinach, arugula) low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Collard greens, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Collards, cooked); FODMAP: collard greens low FODMAP (dietitian lists). Low-FODMAP high-fiber option

name: Bell pepper, red
source: USDA FDC (Peppers, sweet, red); FODMAP: red bell pepper low FODMAP at ~75 g (dietitian low-FODMAP lists confirmed by web search)

name: Bell pepper, green
source: USDA FDC (Peppers, sweet, green); FODMAP: green bell pepper low FODMAP at 75 g, amber at 250 g (web search, dietitian lists)

name: Cucumber
source: USDA FDC (Cucumber, with peel); FODMAP: cucumber low FODMAP (dietitian low-FODMAP lists)

name: Zucchini
source: USDA FDC (Squash, zucchini); FODMAP: zucchini low at ~50 g but high in fructans at ~85 g+ (web search, dietitian lists). Marked moderate since 65 g is near the threshold

name: Tomato, fresh
source: USDA FDC (Tomatoes, red, raw); FODMAP: common/plum tomato low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Cherry/grape tomatoes
source: USDA FDC (Tomatoes, cherry); FODMAP: cherry tomatoes low FODMAP at small servings (dietitian lists); note larger servings of cherry tomatoes rise in fructose

name: Canned diced tomatoes
source: USDA FDC (Tomatoes, canned, diced); FODMAP: plain canned tomatoes low FODMAP at 1/2 cup (dietitian lists); check label for added onion/garlic

name: Green beans
source: USDA FDC (Beans, snap, green); FODMAP: green beans low FODMAP at ~15 beans/75 g (dietitian low-FODMAP lists)

name: Eggplant
source: USDA FDC (Eggplant, raw); FODMAP: eggplant low FODMAP at ~75 g (web search, dietitian lists). Low-FODMAP high-fiber option

name: Celery
source: FODMAP: celery low FODMAP only at small servings (~1/4 stalk); larger amounts high in mannitol (polyol). Fructose/fructans low at small amounts; marked moderate conservatively at 1 stalk (dietitian lists)

name: Carrot
source: USDA FDC (Carrots, raw); FODMAP: carrots low FODMAP at all common servings (dietitian low-FODMAP lists)

name: Mushrooms, button/white
source: FODMAP: button mushrooms are high in MANNITOL (polyol, out of scope) but low in fructose/fructans (dietitian lists; web search). For this user's fructose/fructans-only scope, marked low - but app may flag separately if polyol tracking is added

name: Broccoli
source: FODMAP: broccoli contains fructans; heads low at ~3/4 cup, stalks higher (web search, dietitian lists). Marked moderate conservatively

name: Cauliflower
source: FODMAP: cauliflower is high in mannitol AND moderate-high in fructans; considered high FODMAP even at small servings (web search; Cleveland Clinic; dietitian lists). Marked high conservatively

name: Asparagus
source: FODMAP: asparagus is high in both excess fructose and fructans even at small servings (Cleveland Clinic; Healthline; dietitian high-FODMAP lists). Offender to flag - appears in meal plan

name: Brussels sprouts
source: FODMAP: Brussels sprouts low FODMAP at ~2 sprouts but high in fructans at larger servings (dietitian lists). Marked moderate conservatively at 1/2 cup

name: Cabbage, common green
source: USDA FDC (Cabbage, raw); FODMAP: common green cabbage low FODMAP at ~3/4 cup (dietitian low-FODMAP lists). Savoy cabbage differs

name: Sprouts (alfalfa)
source: USDA FDC (Alfalfa sprouts); FODMAP: alfalfa sprouts low FODMAP (dietitian low-FODMAP lists)

name: Jalapeno pepper
source: USDA FDC (Peppers, jalapeno); FODMAP: chili/jalapeno low FODMAP at typical servings (dietitian low-FODMAP lists)

name: Ginger, fresh
source: USDA FDC (Ginger root, raw); FODMAP: ginger low FODMAP (dietitian low-FODMAP lists)

name: Parsley, fresh
source: USDA FDC (Parsley, fresh); FODMAP: fresh herbs (parsley) low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Basil, fresh
source: USDA FDC (Basil, fresh); FODMAP: fresh basil low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Cilantro, fresh
source: USDA FDC (Cilantro/coriander, fresh); FODMAP: cilantro low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Beets, cooked
source: FODMAP: beetroot is high in fructans; low only at ~2 slices/20 g, high at 1/2 cup (web search; dietitian lists). Marked high conservatively - appears in meal plan

name: Potato, white, with skin, baked
source: USDA FDC (Potato, baked, flesh and skin); FODMAP: potato low FODMAP (Cleveland Clinic; dietitian lists). Low-FODMAP high-fiber when skin-on

name: Sweet potato, baked, with skin
source: FODMAP: sweet potato low FODMAP only at ~1/2 cup/75 g; higher servings high in mannitol (polyol) and fructans (web search; dietitian lists). Marked moderate conservatively

name: Corn, sweet, cooked
source: FODMAP: sweet corn low only at ~1/2 cob/38 g; 1/2 cup kernels is moderate-high in fructans/sorbitol (web search; dietitian lists). Marked moderate conservatively

name: Butternut squash, cooked
source: FODMAP: butternut squash low at ~1/4 cup/45 g; higher servings high in mannitol/fructans (dietitian lists). Marked moderate conservatively at 1/2 cup

name: Spaghetti squash, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Squash, spaghetti, cooked); FODMAP: spaghetti squash low FODMAP at ~1 cup/150 g (dietitian low-FODMAP lists)

name: Parsnip, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Parsnips, cooked); FODMAP: parsnip low FODMAP (dietitian low-FODMAP lists). Low-FODMAP high-fiber option

name: Turnip, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Turnips, cooked); FODMAP: turnip low FODMAP at 1/2 cup (dietitian low-FODMAP lists)

name: Oats, rolled, dry
source: USDA FDC (Oats, rolled); FODMAP: rolled oats low FODMAP at 1/2 cup dry (Cleveland Clinic; dietitian lists). Flagship low-FODMAP high-fiber food

name: Quinoa, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Quinoa, cooked); FODMAP: quinoa low FODMAP at 1 cup (Cleveland Clinic; dietitian lists). Low-FODMAP high-fiber food

name: Brown rice, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Rice, brown, cooked); FODMAP: brown rice low FODMAP (Cleveland Clinic; dietitian lists)

name: White rice, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Rice, white, cooked); FODMAP: white rice low FODMAP (dietitian lists). Note: refined, not whole grain, but used in meal-plan rice dishes

name: Farro, cooked
source: FODMAP: farro is a wheat species high in fructans; low only at ~1/4 cup cooked (dietitian lists). Marked high conservatively at 1/2 cup

name: Pearl barley, cooked
source: FODMAP: barley is high in fructans (and GOS); high FODMAP at typical servings (web search PMC fructan study; dietitian lists). Marked high - appears in meal-plan barley soup

name: Bulgur wheat, cooked
source: FODMAP: bulgur is wheat, high in fructans; low only at ~1/4 cup cooked/typical low serving (web search PMC fructan study; dietitian lists). Marked high at 1/2 cup - appears in meal-plan tabbouleh

name: Whole grain wheat bread
source: FODMAP: whole grain wheat bread is high in fructans (bran/germ); 1 slice/24 g is the max low serving, more is high (web search PMC; dietitian lists). Marked high - heavily used in meal plan

name: White wheat bread
source: FODMAP: regular wheat white bread low at 1 slice/35 g, high at 2 slices (web search; dietitian lists). Marked moderate conservatively

name: Sourdough wheat bread
source: FODMAP: traditional sourdough (long ferment) breaks down fructans; low FODMAP at ~2 slices/97 g (web search; dietitian lists). A useful low-fructan bread swap

name: Whole grain pita bread
source: FODMAP: wheat pita is high in fructans like other wheat breads (dietitian lists). Marked high - appears in meal-plan pita pockets/mezze

name: Whole wheat tortilla
source: FODMAP: wheat tortilla high in fructans (dietitian lists). Marked high - appears in meal-plan veggie wrap/quesadilla

name: Corn tortilla
source: USDA FDC (Tortilla, corn); FODMAP: corn tortillas low FODMAP at 2 (dietitian lists). Low-FODMAP high-fiber wheat-free swap

name: Whole grain English muffin
source: FODMAP: wheat English muffin high in fructans (dietitian lists). Marked high - appears in meal-plan Eggs Benny

name: Whole grain crackers
source: FODMAP: most wheat crackers contain fructans; some rice/seed crackers are low. Marked moderate conservatively (depends on brand; check label)

name: Rice cakes, plain
source: USDA FDC (Rice cakes, plain); FODMAP: plain rice cakes low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Whole grain pasta, cooked
source: FODMAP: wheat pasta low only at ~1/2 cup cooked/74 g; 1 cup is high in fructans (FODMAP Friendly; PMC wholegrain pasta study). Marked high at 1 cup serving

name: Banana, ripe
source: FODMAP: RIPE/spotty banana is high in fructans; low only at ~1/3 medium (web search; Monash/dietitian lists). Marked high for a whole ripe banana - appears in meal plan

name: Banana, unripe/firm
source: FODMAP: firm/unripe (slightly green) banana is low FODMAP at 1 medium (web search; dietitian lists). Low-FODMAP high-fiber when unripe

name: Blueberries
source: USDA FDC (Blueberries, raw); FODMAP: blueberries low FODMAP (web search; dietitian lists)

name: Strawberries
source: USDA FDC (Strawberries, raw); FODMAP: strawberries low FODMAP (web search; dietitian lists). Low-FODMAP high-fiber at 1 cup

name: Raspberries
source: USDA FDC (Raspberries, raw); FODMAP: raspberries low FODMAP at ~1/3 cup (web search; dietitian lists). Flagship low-FODMAP high-fiber fruit

name: Mixed berries
source: USDA FDC (berries, approximate); FODMAP: blueberries/strawberries/raspberries are all low FODMAP at moderate servings (dietitian lists). Low-FODMAP high-fiber

name: Kiwifruit
source: USDA FDC (Kiwifruit, green, raw); FODMAP: kiwi low FODMAP at 2 small (web search; dietitian lists). Near low-FODMAP high-fiber (2.1 g)

name: Orange
source: USDA FDC (Oranges, raw); FODMAP: orange low FODMAP at 1 medium (Cleveland Clinic; dietitian lists). Low-FODMAP high-fiber fruit

name: Pineapple, fresh
source: USDA FDC (Pineapple, raw); FODMAP: fresh pineapple low FODMAP at 1 cup (web search; dietitian lists)

name: Cantaloupe
source: USDA FDC (Melons, cantaloupe); FODMAP: cantaloupe low FODMAP at ~3/4 cup (web search; dietitian lists)

name: Grapes
source: FODMAP: grapes were retested and are now HIGH in excess fructose (web search; dietitian lists). Flagged - appears in meal plan (Snack Pack, Wine N Cheese)

name: Cranberries, dried
source: FODMAP: dried cranberries low at 1 tbsp but moderate-high in fructose at larger servings; usually sweetened (dietitian lists). Marked moderate conservatively

name: Apple
source: FODMAP: apple is high in excess fructose (and sorbitol); classic high-FODMAP fruit (Cleveland Clinic; Healthline; web search). Flagged - appears in meal plan

name: Pear
source: FODMAP: pear is high in excess fructose and sorbitol; classic high-FODMAP fruit (Cleveland Clinic; Healthline; web search). Flagged

name: Mango
source: FODMAP: mango is high in excess fructose (web search; Healthline; dietitian lists). Flagged

name: Watermelon
source: FODMAP: watermelon is high in excess fructose, fructans AND mannitol; one of the highest-FODMAP fruits (Cleveland Clinic; Healthline; web search). Flagged

name: Dried fruit (raisins/mixed)
source: FODMAP: dried fruit concentrates fructose; high FODMAP (excess fructose) at typical servings (web search; Monash/dietitian high-FODMAP lists). Flagged

name: Lemon, whole/wedge
source: USDA FDC (Lemon, raw); FODMAP: lemon low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Lime, whole/wedge
source: USDA FDC (Lime, raw); FODMAP: lime low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Honey
source: USDA FDC (Honey); FODMAP: honey is high in excess fructose (web search; Cleveland Clinic; Healthline). Primary offender to flag - appears in meal plan

name: Maple syrup, pure
source: USDA FDC (Syrups, maple); FODMAP: pure maple syrup low FODMAP at 1 tbsp (dietitian lists) - a recommended honey swap. (Still added sugar for NOOM/DASH)

name: High-fructose corn syrup
source: USDA FDC (Syrups, corn, high-fructose); FODMAP: HFCS is concentrated excess fructose; high FODMAP (web search; Healthline). Primary offender to flag

name: Agave syrup
source: USDA FDC (Sweeteners, agave syrup); FODMAP: agave is very high in fructose; high FODMAP (web search; dietitian high-FODMAP lists). Offender to flag

name: Table sugar (sucrose)
source: USDA FDC (Sugars, granulated); FODMAP: sucrose is glucose+fructose balanced, low FODMAP in small amounts (dietitian lists). Still added sugar for NOOM/DASH

name: Brown sugar
source: USDA FDC (Sugars, brown); FODMAP: brown sugar low FODMAP in small amounts like sucrose (dietitian lists)

name: Sugar-free jam
source: FODMAP unknown: sugar-free jams may use polyols (sorbitol/maltitol) or fruit concentrates; varies by brand. Not verified safe

name: Dark chocolate
source: USDA FDC (Chocolate, dark 70-85%); FODMAP: dark chocolate low FODMAP at ~30 g/5 squares (dietitian lists)

name: Dark chocolate chips
source: USDA FDC (chocolate chips, dark); FODMAP: dark chocolate low FODMAP at small servings (dietitian lists)

name: Air-popped popcorn
source: USDA FDC (Snacks, popcorn, air-popped); FODMAP: popcorn low FODMAP at ~7 cups (dietitian lists). Low-FODMAP high-fiber whole grain

name: Granola
source: USDA FDC (granola, approximate); FODMAP unknown: granola often contains honey, dried fruit, wheat, and nuts that raise fructose/fructans; varies by brand. Not verified safe

name: Protein powder (whey isolate)
source: FODMAP: whey protein isolate is low FODMAP (lactose removed; dietitian lists). Concentrate or added inulin/HFCS would differ - this row is isolate

name: Lactose-free/almond milk, unsweetened
source: USDA FDC (Almond milk, unsweetened); FODMAP: unsweetened almond milk low FODMAP at 1 cup (dietitian lists). Sweetened or oat milk would differ

name: Cow's milk, low-fat
source: USDA FDC (Milk, lowfat 1%); FODMAP: cow's milk is high in LACTOSE (out of scope for this user) but low in fructose/fructans. Marked low for fructose/fructans-only scope

name: Vegetable broth, low-sodium
source: FODMAP: most vegetable/chicken broths and stocks contain onion and garlic (high fructans); marked high conservatively. Low-FODMAP certified broths exist as a swap

name: Chicken broth, low-sodium
source: FODMAP: standard chicken broth/stock contains onion and garlic (high fructans); marked high conservatively. Certified low-FODMAP broths are a swap

name: Miso paste
source: USDA FDC (Miso); FODMAP: miso low FODMAP at 2 tbsp (dietitian lists). High sodium flag for DASH

name: Baking powder
source: USDA FDC (Leavening agents, baking powder); FODMAP: baking powder low FODMAP in cooking amounts (dietitian lists). High sodium flag

name: Salt
source: USDA FDC (Salt, table); FODMAP: salt has no fructose/fructans. Major sodium source for DASH tracking

name: Black pepper, ground
source: USDA FDC (Spices, pepper, black); FODMAP: black pepper low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Dried oregano
source: USDA FDC (Spices, oregano, dried); FODMAP: dried oregano low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Dried thyme
source: USDA FDC (Spices, thyme, dried); FODMAP: dried thyme low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Bay leaf
source: USDA FDC (Spices, bay leaf); FODMAP: bay leaf low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Chili flakes / red pepper flakes
source: USDA FDC (Spices, red/cayenne pepper); FODMAP: chili flakes low FODMAP in small amounts (dietitian lists)

name: Sea salt + pepper seasoning
source: USDA FDC (Salt); FODMAP: salt and pepper have no fructose/fructans. High sodium flag for DASH

name: Garlic-infused oil
source: FODMAP: fructans are NOT oil-soluble, so garlic-infused oil is low FODMAP and a recommended way to get garlic flavor safely (Monash; Cleveland Clinic; dietitian lists)

name: Carbonated/seltzer water
source: USDA FDC (Carbonated water); FODMAP: plain carbonated water has no fructose/fructans (dietitian lists)

name: Herbal tea (peppermint)
source: FODMAP: peppermint and most plain herbal teas are low FODMAP; chamomile and oolong are high. This row is peppermint (dietitian lists)

name: Coffee, black
source: USDA FDC (Coffee, brewed); FODMAP: black coffee low FODMAP (dietitian lists)

name: Orange juice
source: FODMAP: orange juice low at ~1/2 cup but fructose load rises quickly without fiber; marked moderate conservatively (dietitian lists). Whole orange preferred

name: Applesauce, unsweetened
source: FODMAP: applesauce is concentrated apple, high in excess fructose and sorbitol (dietitian high-FODMAP lists). Flagged

name: Pumpkin puree, canned
source: FODMAP: canned pumpkin low at ~1/4 cup/75 g but higher servings rise in fructans/mannitol (dietitian lists). Marked moderate conservatively at 1/2 cup

name: Cooking spray (oil)
source: USDA FDC (oil cooking spray); FODMAP: pure oil spray has no fructose/fructans

name: Steel-cut oats, dry
source: USDA FDC (Oats); FODMAP: steel-cut oats low FODMAP at ~1/4 cup dry (dietitian lists). Low-FODMAP high-fiber food

name: Cottage cheese, low-fat
source: USDA FDC (Cheese, cottage, lowfat); FODMAP: cottage cheese low in fructose/fructans (lactose out of scope). Low-FODMAP at ~2 tbsp per Monash but fructose/fructans stay low at 1/2 cup

name: Canned salmon, drained
source: USDA FDC (Fish, salmon, canned); FODMAP: plain canned salmon free of fructose/fructans

name: Pork tenderloin, cooked
source: USDA FDC (Pork, tenderloin, cooked); FODMAP: plain pork free of fructose/fructans

name: Beef steak (sirloin), cooked
source: USDA FDC (Beef, sirloin, cooked); FODMAP: plain beef free of fructose/fructans

name: Quinoa, dry
source: USDA FDC (Quinoa, uncooked); FODMAP: quinoa low FODMAP (dietitian lists). Low-FODMAP high-fiber food
